a260718p1.summary: Japan is planning to buy 27,500 next-generation Rubin chips from Nvidia Corp. to build a homegrown foundational AI model for robots.
a260718p1.title: Japan plans to buy 27,500 Nvidia Rubin chips to develop a domestic AI foundation model for robots, in a Noetra-led effort that includes SoftBank, Sony, and NEC (Bloomberg)

a260718p10.summary: Last year, Microsoft's 13-inch Surface Laptop quickly became one of my favorite thin-and-light Windows notebooks.
a260718p10.title: A review of Microsoft's 13-inch Surface Laptop shows 8GB of RAM is not enough for a good Windows 11 experience; PC makers have unveiled upcoming 8GB laptops (Antonio G. Di Benedetto/The Verge)

a260718p11.summary: Opponents of the rapid buildout of data centers plan to hold protests on Saturday in at least 125 locations across the United States …
a260718p11.title: The grassroots group HumansFirst is organizing protests against the "unaccountable" buildout of data centers across 125 US locations on July 18 (Reuters)

a260718p12.summary: In January 2025, just three days after entering office for his second term, President Donald Trump signed …
a260718p12.title: How the Trump administration shifted from a "light-touch" approach to AI policy to an interventionist stance that led to restrictions on top AI models in the US (Leo Schwartz/The Information)

a260718p13.summary: White House officials are grappling with how deeply political betting has worked itself into fabric of government
a260718p13.title: Prediction market political betting on insider info spreads in DC; sources: WH lawyers raised alarms over anonymous Polymarket bets on the Iran ceasefire timing (Wall Street Journal)

a260718p14.summary: Building an AI supercampus increasingly means paying more than you bargained for. Oracle's attempt to salvage a proposed $165 billion project …
a260718p14.title: Permit hurdles push up costs for AI data centers; Oracle pivoted from gas turbines to costlier fuel cells for its Project Jupiter in NM, costing billions more (Ann Davis Vaughan/The Information)

a260718p15.summary: Incoming PM will reallocate unspecified resources from unpopular plan to helping with cost of living
a260718p15.title: UK's incoming PM Andy Burnham is expected to scrap Keir Starmer's plans for digital ID cards; last year a petition opposing the cards attracted ~3M signatures (Geraldine McKelvie/The Guardian)

a260718p16.summary: If you're like me, you or a loved one has struggled through the process of gaining pre-approval for the medical care that your physician has recommended.
a260718p16.title: A look at AI's potential impact on insurance-coverage decisions like prior authorization as the Trump admin starts to pilot using AI to evaluate Medicare claims (Joshua Cohen/Ars Technica)

a260718p17.summary: An investigation by 9to5Mac reveals dozens of apps that disguise gambling platforms as simple games and utilities.
a260718p17.title: Analysis finds 60+ "jacket apps" on the App Store disguised as simple games and utilities that become gambling apps when accessed from Brazilian IP addresses (9to5Mac)

a260718p18.summary: The firm's chip unit T-Head aims to lower migration barriers to Zhenwu AI computing architectures, following similar initiatives by Huawei and Moore Threads
a260718p18.title: Alibaba open-sources its chip software, following similar plays from Huawei and Moore Threads, as Chinese GPU makers try to break the dominance of Nvidia's CUDA (Ann Cao/South China Morning Post)

a260718p2.summary: Sunny Gupta has led two prior enterprise tech companies with backing from venture capital firm Madrona in the past 20 years. iConclude sold to Opsware.
a260718p2.title: Thira, an AI startup founded by Apptio co-founders to develop AI agents that handle back-office tasks such as IT support, raised a $21M seed led by Madrona (Todd Bishop/GeekWire)

a260718p3.summary: On a video conference call from Hangzhou earlier this year, one of China's hottest startups held a four-hour pitch meeting …
a260718p3.title: Sources: China's National AI Industry Investment Fund gained voting rights in DeepSeek by joining its $7.4B round; other investors like Tencent and JD got none (Bloomberg)

a260718p4.summary: The U.S. telemetry company will establish a Tel Aviv office after buying the AI-powered detection engineering startup.
a260718p4.title: Data infrastructure startup Cribl acquires CardinalOps, which offers AI-powered threat detection tools and had raised $40M, sources say for around $100M (Meir Orbach/CTech)

a260718p5.summary: The British firm has built a collaborative platform to help organizations address supply chain security risks.
a260718p5.title: London-based Risk Ledger, which helps organizations manage supply chain cyber risks, raised a £24M Series B led by Axiom Equity, taking total funding to £33.8M (Ionut Arghire/SecurityWeek)

a260718p6.summary: Every month, a Beijing-based ByteDance employee says he burns through close to a billion units of a new corporate currency - one that cannot buy a coffee or pay rent.
a260718p6.title: China's National Data Administration says the country's daily AI token consumption hit 140T in March 2026, up from 100T in December 2025 and 100B in early 2024 (Minxiao Chang/South China Morning Post)

a260718p7.summary: Feathery, an AI operating and decisioning platform for financial services firms, has secured $30m in funding as it expands …
a260718p7.title: Feathery, which develops an AI operating and decisioning system for financial services, raised $30M in total funding, including a recently completed Series A (FinTech Global)

a260718p8.summary: Videogame retailer GameStop (GME.N) owns nearly 10% of e-commerce company eBay, the company said in a regulatory filing late on Friday …
a260718p8.title: SEC filing: GameStop says it owns 9.8% of eBay, signaling its intent to press ahead with a bid for the company after its unsolicited $56B offer was rejected (Reuters)

a260718p9.summary: AISI has tracked the cyber capabilities of frontier AI models since 2023. On our evaluations, the most capable models …
a260718p9.title: Analysis: recent open weight models lag frontier closed models' cyber capabilities by 4 to 7 months, a narrower gap than the 6 to 10 months through most of 2025 (AI Security Institute)
